description: Hierarchical model-predictive control: the planner imagines macro/primitive edit sequences with the world model (CEM; energy = latent_l1 + status + error terms) and grounds only the chosen prefix in real Lean. Equal-budget downstream proving (Lean-call budget sweep 64/128/256/512) plus HWM-style ablations (hierarchy on/off, failure-dynamics on/off, action-algebra coverage, k_replan sweep 1/2/4/8, horizon 1/2/4/6).
motivation: Run the headline equal-budget WM-planner vs model-free comparison and isolate WHERE world-model planning helps.
formal_results: Planned - src/planner and src/eval are empty stubs; planner.yaml holds first-cut HWM Franka Table-10 hyperparameters pending a bake-off. No results.
conclusions: Hypotheses H5/H6/H7/H8: hierarchy helps long-horizon, WM-planner beats baseline at equal budget, the advantage concentrates in long proofs, and grounding controls compounding error. Untested.
